changePlan.status: Draft change plan; vectors authored ahead of the 0.8 document edit
changePlan.sha256Note: Refreshed after the sequencing section; refresh deliberately on any further plan edit.

conventions.sectionReferences: Section numbers refer to Architecture 0.7 sections, per the change plan.
conventions.unknown: U denotes the three-valued Unknown produced by an unrecognised or unevaluatable atom (change C7).

given: Root grants Capability P to Actor A with liveness-scoped validity maintained by lease L-A
A derives Capability C for Actor B from P
Lease L-A has expired
when: B presents C to the target
then: The Execution is denied: every liveness-scoped link in the chain must be live at authorisation time

given: A chain as in BR-08-ADV-C1-001 with lease L-A active
The target's authorisation boundary has no means to evaluate the liveness of L-A
when: B presents C to the target
then: The Execution is denied under the fail-closed rule
The decision uses a deterministic, non-sensitive diagnostic category

given: A chain as in BR-08-ADV-C1-001 with lease L-A active and evaluatable at the target
when: B presents C within all other Constraints
then: The Execution is authorised

given: A device holds a genesis-grade Capability permitting assertion of Origin.Device
The device delegates a Capability to companion software
when: The companion software emits an occurrence asserting Origin.Device under the delegated Capability
then: The assertion is refused: a delegated Capability asserts at most Origin.Derived
The occurrence carries at most Origin.Derived, or unverified where no assertion right is exercised

given: A device holds a genesis-grade Capability permitting assertion of Origin.Device
when: The device itself emits an occurrence asserting Origin.Device
then: The assertion succeeds and the origin class is recorded

given: A Capability with wall-clock validity is presented before its not-after instant
The Execution is authorised and its effect is in progress
when: The not-after instant passes while the effect is still in progress
then: Base semantics do not interrupt, terminate, or retroactively deny the effect
The effect remains governed by the withdrawal and cancellation semantics of 10.3

given: The same Capability after its not-after instant
when: A new Execution is presented
then: The Execution is denied

given: Root grants Capability G with Constraint K to Actor A
A derives C1 for B adding no Constraint; B derives C2 for D adding no Constraint
The domain representation does not inline ancestor Constraints in C2
An Execution is prepared that violates K
when: D presents C2 to the target
then: The Execution is denied: the grandparent Constraint K participates in the effective conjunction
An implementation that evaluates only C2's own added Constraints fails this vector and does not conform

given: Actor A holds Capability P carrying a quantified Constraint rate <= N per window with default chain-occurrence pooling
A derives sibling Capabilities C-1 and C-2 for two holders
when: Holder 1 performs N authorised Executions under C-1 within one window
Holder 2 presents an Execution under C-2 within the same window
then: Holder 2's Execution is denied: both siblings draw on the single budget at the Constraint's occurrence in P

given: A Capability carrying rate <= N per window with budget fully available
A series of Executions that are denied for an unrelated reason (an unsatisfied non-quantified Constraint)
when: After the denials, a fully satisfying Execution is presented within the same window
then: The Execution is authorised: denied Executions consume nothing; bookkeeping changes only on successful authorisation

given: A quantified Constraint whose vocabulary declares a per-holder accounting scope
An evaluator that cannot enforce the declared scope
when: An Execution presenting the Capability is evaluated
then: The Execution is denied under the fail-closed rule: a declared accounting scope the evaluator cannot enforce is unevaluatable

given: Actor A holds an unadorned Capability X carrying no Constraint restricting Delegation
when: A derives Capability Y for Actor B from X
then: The Delegation succeeds and B may execute within Y's effective authority: Capabilities are delegable by default

given: Actor A holds Capability X carrying a Constraint restricting further Delegation
A purported derivation Y for Actor B exists despite the restriction
when: B presents Y to the target
then: The Execution is denied: the Delegation-restricting Constraint conjoins along the chain and invalidates the derivation's authority
The same holds for any deeper descendant of Y

given: A Capability carrying Not(U) where U is an unrecognised atom
when: An Execution presenting the Capability is evaluated
then: Not(Unknown) is Unknown; the Execution is denied

given: A Capability carrying AnyOf(S, U) where S is a recognised, satisfied atom and U is unrecognised
when: An Execution presenting the Capability is evaluated
then: AnyOf with a True member is True; the Execution is authorised

given: A Capability carrying AllOf(S, U) where S is a recognised, satisfied atom and U is unrecognised
when: An Execution presenting the Capability is evaluated
then: AllOf(True, Unknown) is Unknown; the Execution is denied

given: A Capability carrying AnyOf(U, F) where U is unrecognised and F is a recognised, unsatisfied atom
when: An Execution presenting the Capability is evaluated
then: AnyOf(Unknown, False) is Unknown; the Execution is denied

given: A Capability carrying AllOf(F, U) where F is a recognised, unsatisfied atom and U is unrecognised
when: An Execution presenting the Capability is evaluated
then: AllOf with a False member is False; the Execution is denied
Short-circuit evaluation on False is permitted because the result is identical

given: A Capability carrying AnyOf(X, Not(X)) where X is a single unrecognised atom appearing twice
when: An Execution presenting the Capability is evaluated
then: The expression is Unknown and the Execution is denied
Implementations MUST NOT recognise the classical tautology: evaluation is structural, with no reasoning across repeated atoms
vectors.notes: The decisive divergence trap: a supervaluating implementation authorises here and is nonconforming.

given: A candidate whose Definition Constraint is AnyOf(U, M) where M is a recognised, matching atom and U is unrecognised
when: A resolver evaluates the candidate
then: AnyOf with a True member is True; the candidate is retained
The resolver records the Unknown atom in its explanatory record
vectors.notes: Inverts the 0.7 poisoning-era example in 29.2; the inversion is deliberate and recorded in change C7.

given: A candidate whose Definition Constraint is AllOf(M, U) where M is a recognised, matching atom and U is unrecognised
when: A resolver evaluates the candidate
then: AllOf(True, Unknown) is Unknown; the candidate is excluded
The resolver records the exclusion and the Unknown atom

given: A Constraint type whose value Shape version 2 adds an optional constituent carrying narrowing semantics
A delegator narrows using a version 2 value
An evaluator that recognises only version 1 of the value Shape
when: An Execution presenting the Capability is evaluated
then: The atom is Unknown: Constraint evaluation is exempt from additive Shape projection
Standing alone, the Unknown atom denies the Execution
The evaluator MUST NOT evaluate the version 1 projection of the Constraint value

given: The same version-skewed evaluator
A Capability carrying AnyOf(NewConstraint-v2, OldConstraint-v1) where the version 1 atom is recognised and satisfied
when: An Execution presenting the Capability is evaluated
then: The Execution is authorised through the authored fallback branch: the migration pattern under three-valued evaluation

given: An Operation input value shaped as version 2 of an open payload Shape, carrying an added optional constituent
A consumer that recognises only version 1
when: The value is delivered as an Operation input
then: The consumer accepts and processes the version 1 canonical projection and ignores the unknown structure
The projection exemption is scoped to Constraint values only; payload-plane compatibility is unchanged

given: A Capability carrying a Constraint whose declaration is well-formed but whose type the evaluator has decided not to implement
when: An Execution presenting the Capability is evaluated
then: The Execution is denied
The decision identifies the declined Constraint type by canonical name in a deterministic, non-sensitive diagnostic
Declining is a decision with defined semantics; inability to identify the well-formed declaration is nonconformance

given: A vocabulary revision that changes a Constraint type's evaluation semantics or value Shape while retaining its canonical name
when: The vocabulary revision is checked
then: The revision is nonconforming: authority-plane evolution requires a new canonical name and authored AnyOf fallback

given: An implementation claiming conformance
when: Its evidence is assembled
then: The implementation declares its Constraint-type recognition set; a static domain satisfies this with a compile-time declared set

given: A provider Actor holding authority over a resource space exposes a creating Operation
when: An authorised Execution creates a new resource and the requester receives a Capability over it
then: The issued Capability's derivation chain terminates, through the provider's authority, in a primordial grant
The issuance is recorded as a Delegation like any other

given: A provider whose own resource-space authority is bounded by a Constraint
when: An Execution requests creation such that the issued Capability would exceed the provider's effective authority
then: The issuance is refused: a provider cannot issue authority exceeding what its own chain permits

given: A stack's delivery ledger
when: Its evidence is assembled
then: The ledger records the chain-conjunction representation choice (carried, pre-evaluated, or resolved)
The ledger records the implied revocation ceiling as an operational property

given: Actor A holds Capability C
A Terminus occurrence retires A under the domain's declared disposition policy
when: Any participant attempts an Execution presenting C
then: The Execution is denied: a Capability held by a terminated Actor cannot authorise, since no surviving participant is its designated holder

given: Actor A granted an immortal Capability C to Actor B under a disposition policy in which the grant survives
A is retired by a Terminus occurrence
when: B presents C and provenance is inspected
then: The Execution is evaluated normally
Provenance remains attributable to A's recorded identity at grant time; the grantor is named even though no longer reachable

given: A domain supporting dynamic Actors
when: Its evidence is assembled
then: The domain declares its Terminus disposition policy: held Capabilities, outbound grants and their survival schedule, and reference retirement consistent with 9.1

coverage.C13: Documentation-only change (legibility scope statement and extension direction); no behavioural vector. The guaranteed surface is the normative text per 29.3.
coverage.C14: Documentation-only change (open question recorded); no behavioural vector.
